items.title: Global uncertainty a reason to 'press ahead' with urgent reform, Albanese says

children.content: The upheaval sparked by the global oil crisis only heightens the case for economic reform, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese will say, after delivering a rare address to the nation.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese delivers address to the nation.

articles.title: Trump says he's considering pulling US out of 'paper tiger' NATO

children.content: Donald Trump says he is strongly considering pulling the United States out of NATO after allies failed to back US military action against Iran, in an interview with the UK's Daily Telegraph.

image.altText: A man in a suit and tie sits at a desk

gallery.altText: Albanese, standing in an office and wearing a navy suit and light blue tie, looks at the camera

items.title: Trump says Iran asked for a ceasefire and US will consider if strait is reopened

children.content: The US president said America is "blasting Iran into oblivion" and claimed a senior figure from the regime has made contact.

image.altText: A close up of Donald Trump.

items.title: PM says next months 'may not be easy', urges Australians to save fuel

children.content: Anthony Albanese asks Australians to trade their cars for public transport if they can, while reassuring those who need to drive they can continue to do so amid the global oil crisis.

image.altText: A man in a suit sits at a desk and reads a teleprompter, looking in to a camera.

items.title: One important man was missing from Albanese's national address

children.content: The prime minister risks criticism for using the rare measure of a national address to urge calm without announcing any new measures.

image.altText: Albanese, standing in an office and wearing a navy suit and light blue tie, looks at the camera

items.title: 'Ridiculous': How Australians are coping with sky-high fuel prices

children.content: The ABC asked how you were coping with fuel prices and here's what we heard back.

image.altText: A composite image of two men and two women.

items.title: Keep track of today's sharp drop in petrol prices around the country

children.content: The federal government's cut to fuel excise has almost immediately flowed through to petrol prices at the pump, according to ABC tracking of fuel price data nationwide.

articles.title: Fuel prices begin to fall at petrol stations — quicker than expected

children.content: Consumers were warned the fuel excise cut might take weeks to be passed on to retailers, but prices have already begun to fall, NRMA says.

image.altText: petrol prices

articles.title: Australia assessing ways to help Pacific nations that 'basically run on diesel'

children.content: Pacific nations are not yet suffering large-scale petrol shortages but are already starting to grapple with the economic impacts of the war, with fuel price hikes threatening to drive up inflation.

image.altText: Close up of BP petrol pump going into a car.

items.title: WA man charged with terrorism offences wrote of Bondi attacks in diary, court told

children.content: Jayson Joseph Michaels, 20, had reflected on the Bondi attacks in his diary and was allegedly planning a "day of justice", a Perth court hears.

image.altText: A smiling youg man in high-vis in front of a digger holding a hose.

items.title: Why it's a 'no-brainer' that teachers are fleeing this broken system

children.content: Victoria bills itself as the 'education state' but a growing exodus of teachers and underfunding has that tag under threat.

image.altText: A woman sits on a sofa.

items.title: Arnold carried in jubilant scenes after Iraq qualifies for World Cup

children.content: Former Socceroos coach Graham Arnold has guided Iraq to their first FIFA World Cup appearance in 40 years with a playoff win over Bolivia.

items.title: Study shows gen Z craves in-person connection more than other generations

children.content: Gen Z is embracing retro technology like iPods, vinyl and Polaroid cameras and craves in-person connection more than any other generation, according to a Live Nation study.

items.title: Sixers force NBL title decider in Game 4 cliffhanger

items.title: Inside Queensland's first recovery village for eating disorder survivors

children.content: A couple whose daughters survived eating disorders is opening a first-of-its-kind recovery village to help survivors ease back into their normal lives without relapsing.

items.title: How this Aussie went from TAFE to the head of Sony character animation

items.title: Dezi Freeman investigation to probe January bushfires 1km from hiding spot

children.content: Victoria Police have officially confirmed that the person shot dead by officers at a property in Victoria's north-east on Monday morning was fugitive Dezi Freeman.

image.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: National addresses are rare and historic. Now, Albanese has made another

children.content: Pandemics, wars, and landmark moments have been immortalised in addresses to the nation. Anthony Albanese has become one of the few prime ministers to employ the form.

items.title: Dire future of superb fairy-wren could mean catastrophe for other species

children.content: The researchers said the superb fairy-wren would likely go extinct within 30-40 years unless action was taken to stop greenhouse gas emissions causing climate change.

image.altText: A small blue-headed wren sitting in a tree.

items.title: Cultural compensation replaces fine in 'rare' illegal logging ruling

items.title: Australia's solar panel waste issue is growing in size year-on-year

items.title: More than 290,000 visits to Daniel's Law website in first three months
